description: Test scenarios for Cronus agent debugging. Each scenario has intentional bugs, a prompt to send to the agent, and the expected fix behavior.

scenarios.bug: divide() uses integer division (//), should use float division (/)
scenarios.prompt: The divide function in operations.py returns incorrect results for non-integer division. For example, divide(10, 3) returns 3 instead of 3.333... Fix the bug.

scenarios.bug: clear() resets result but doesn't clear history
scenarios.prompt: The Calculator.clear() method doesn't fully reset the calculator state. After calling clear(), get_history() still returns old entries. Fix the clear method.

scenarios.bug: word_count doesn't strip punctuation from words
scenarios.prompt: The word_count function treats 'hello' and 'hello,' as different words because it doesn't strip punctuation. Fix word_count to ignore punctuation when counting.

scenarios.bug: truncate always adds suffix, doesn't account for suffix length in total
scenarios.prompt: The truncate function has two bugs: (1) it adds the suffix even when the text already fits within max_length, and (2) when truncating, the total length exceeds max_length because it doesn't account for the suffix length. Fix both issues.
The title_case function incorrectly capitalizes articles and prepositions like 'of', 'the', 'a', etc. These should stay lowercase unless they are the first word. Fix title_case.
The LinkedList::pushFront method adds the node correctly but doesn't update the size counter. After calling pushFront, size() returns the wrong value. Fix the bug.
scenarios.test_command: cd cpp/linked_list && g++ -std=c++17 -o test test_linked_list.cpp linked_list.cpp && ./test

scenarios.bug: remove() skips checking the head node
scenarios.prompt: The LinkedList::remove method cannot remove the head node. It starts searching from the second element. Fix remove() to handle removing the head node.
scenarios.test_command: cd cpp/linked_list && g++ -std=c++17 -o test test_linked_list.cpp linked_list.cpp && ./test

scenarios.bug: Destructor only deletes head node, leaks the rest
scenarios.prompt: The LinkedList destructor has a memory leak. It only deletes the head node instead of walking the entire list. Fix the destructor to properly free all nodes.
scenarios.test_command: cd cpp/linked_list && g++ -std=c++17 -fsanitize=address -o test test_linked_list.cpp linked_list.cpp && ./test

scenarios.bug: trim() only removes spaces, not tabs or newlines
scenarios.prompt: StringUtils::trim only strips space characters but not tabs or newlines. Fix it to strip all whitespace characters.
scenarios.test_command: cd cpp/string_utils && g++ -std=c++17 -o test test_string_utils.cpp string_utils.cpp && ./test

scenarios.prompt: StringUtils::join adds the delimiter after every element including the last one, so join({"a","b"}, ",") returns "a,b," instead of "a,b". Fix the join function.
scenarios.test_command: cd cpp/string_utils && g++ -std=c++17 -o test test_string_utils.cpp string_utils.cpp && ./test

scenarios.bug: completeTask crashes on nonexistent task ID
scenarios.prompt: TaskManager.completeTask throws a TypeError when called with a nonexistent task ID because it tries to access .status on undefined. Fix it to handle missing tasks gracefully (return null).

scenarios.bug: removeTask doesn't clean up the status index
scenarios.prompt: TaskManager.removeTask deletes the task from the map but doesn't remove it from the internal status index. After removing a task, getTasksByStatus still tries to look it up. Fix removeTask to clean up the index.

scenarios.bug: flatten only goes one level deep
scenarios.prompt: The flatten function only flattens one level of nesting. [[[1]]] returns [[1]] instead of [1]. Fix flatten to recursively flatten all levels.
The groupBy function crashes when the key function returns null or undefined. Items with missing keys should be grouped under an 'unknown' key. Fix groupBy to handle null/undefined keys.
Fix all the bugs in the calculator module. The tests in test_calculator.py define the expected behavior. Run the tests to see what's failing and fix all issues.
The task manager has several bugs. Run the tests to see what's failing and fix all issues in taskManager.js.
